pattern: Two-pointer simulation: advance through the typed string, matching name characters or allowing extra repetitions of the last matched character.
hints: Walk both strings with separate pointers. At each step in typed, ask: does this character continue the current run of the last-matched name character, or start the next one?
Advance the name pointer when typed[j] matches name[i]; advance only j when typed[j] equals the last successfully matched character (long-press repeat). Any other case fails.
After consuming all of typed, make sure you have also consumed all of name — leftover name characters mean the typed string is too short.

edge_inputs.description: single char repeated many times in typed
name and typed identical, no long-press
typed is shorter than name — impossible
single character name, single character typed, match
single character name but wrong char in typed
extra char at end of typed that does not belong to name
runs look correct but the second char of name is over-repeated before reaching it
long-press on middle character with exact run count needed

stress.description: random name up to 200 chars with each character repeated 1-3 times in typed
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    length = max(1, size)
    name = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(length))
    typed = ''
    for c in name:
        typed += c * rng.randint(1, 3)
    return [name, typed]
